name: Wu's Beef B1
recipeInstructions:
1. Thinly slice filet mignon across grain into strips 2 x 1/2 x 1/4-inch.
2. In a mixing bowl, combine 1 tbsp oil, 1 tbsp cornstarch, 1 tbsp dark soy sauce, 2 tsp light soy sauce, Hoisin sauce, cooking wine, 1/4 tsp sugar, salt, and pepper.
3. Marinate the sliced beef in this mixture for 15 minutes.
4. Pour 2 quarts of oil into a deep-fryer and heat to 400 degrees on high heat. Test the readiness by dropping a rice noodle into the oil; if it pops up immediately, the oil is hot enough.
5. Place the package of noodles into the hot oil. They should puff up into a larger size upon contact with the oil. Immediately drain on paper towels.
6. Heat a wok or skillet on high heat and coat the sides and bottom with 1 tbsp oil.
7. Fry onions for 1 minute, then add the beef; cook for an additional 2 minutes. Stir very little.
8. In a separate bowl, combine 1 tsp cornstarch, 1/4 tsp sugar, 1/2 tsp light soy sauce, 1 tsp dark soy sauce, oyster sauce, and 3 tbsp water; stir until smooth.
9. Add the sauce mixture to the beef and onion mix, stirring for 1 minute. Remove from heat.
10. Spoon the beef onto the noodles.
